title: Three offline validation stages — pre-registered (evasive coercion · emergency calibration · swarm fidelity)
scope: All Layer-1, offline, $0, no Anthropic API, no gated dataset. Corpora are AUTHORED, locked representative samples (the confirmatory n>=300/1000 corpora are a future registered-report deliverable). Their SHA-256 is locked in MANIFEST before any run; the runners read thresholds only from this spec.

stages.name: Evasive-coercion (fast-mode baseline)
stages.engine_under_test: fast-mode on-device gate (NO distilled NPU model exists yet)
stages.symmetric_null: If the fast-mode baseline misses the deep-claim thresholds, report the NULL honestly: the 'Ambient-Deep NPU' claim remains UNPROVEN and load-bearing; the system degrades to Fast-Mode+Primitives. No post-hoc adjustment.
stages.runner_passes_if: the runner computes brier + recall and applies this locked rule honestly (PASS or a correctly-reported NULL)

stages.name: Multi-hop digital-swarm communication fidelity
stages.engine_under_test: AIPS/Echo swarm simulation over a dependency-tree graph
stages.pass_if: E ~ U*D fits linearly (R2(E~U*D) > R2(E~ (U*D)^2)) AND fidelity degrades monotonically with hop depth

acceptance: Each stage runs and reports its locked outcome. The suite is GREEN when every stage applies its locked decision rule honestly — including S1 reporting the evasive null.
